methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
PILGRIM is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jake Adler that has raised $4.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private peers and 2 public comparables in the defense technology sector, including Series A companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M), alongside Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $4.3M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $11M valuation. Peer-Median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. These divergent results reflect the wide distribution in defense tech valuations at comparable stages.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $9M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $80M. The substantial gap between methods reflects key uncertainties: stage-progression relies on raised capital as a proxy for current value, potentially understating trajectory, while peer-median is sensitive to the wide dispersion in recent Defense & Security financings and the mix of Series A versus Seed comparables. Revenue traction, proprietary technology differentiation, and near-term funding momentum would be critical inputs for narrowing this range.
